messages.content:
Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which of the following is the best indication for propofol as an intravenous induction agent?
(A) Neurosurgery
(B) Day care surgery
(C) Patients with coronary artery disease
(D) In neonates
B: Day care surgery

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All are components of saint's traid except
(A) Renal stones
(B) Hiatus hernia
(C) Diveiculitis of colon
(D) Gall stones
A: Renal stones

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Von-Rosen's sign is positive in -
(A) Perthe'sdisease
(B) SCFE
(C) DDH
(D) CTEV
C: DDH

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

The striated vertebra is seen in -
(A) TB spine
(B) Haemangioma
(C) Chordoma
(D) Metastasis
B: Haemangioma

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Thyroid storm can be treated by all the following drugs except-
(A) Propylthiouracil
(B) Dexamethasone
(C) Propranolol
(D) Aspirin
D: Aspirin

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Best level of anesthesia for LSCS:
(A) T8
(B) T10
(C) T6
(D) T4
D: T4

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Genital tubercle forms which pa in females ?
(A) Labia major
(B) Labia minora
(C) Clitoris
(D) None
C: Clitoris

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

True about Brenner tumor:a) Usually bilateralb) Resembles fibromac) Accounts for 20% of all ovarian tumorsd) Common in postmenopausal age group
(A) c
(B) bd
(C) ab
(D) ac
B: bd

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Buccolingual positioning of mandibular Canal can be assessed by:
(A) Horizontal 0 and -20 degree.
(B) Horizontal 0 and -15 degree.
(C) Vertical 0 and -20 degree.
(D) Vertical 0 and -15 degree.
C: Vertical 0 and -20 degree.

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Palpable purpura could occur in the following conditions, except -
(A) Thrombocytopenia
(B) Small-vessel vasculitis
(C) Disseminated gonococcal infection
(D) Acute meningococcemia
A: Thrombocytopenia

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Boucher's method of relining is a
(A) Open mouth technique
(B) Chair side relining technique
(C) Functional relining method
(D) Closed mouth relining technique
A: Open mouth technique

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

The progestogenic emergency contraceptive pills act by -
(A) Altered cervical secretion
(B) Inhibition of ovulation
(C) Anti-implantation effect
(D) Inhibition of LH secretion
C: Anti-implantation effect

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

The most common side effect of IUD insertion is -
(A) Bleeding
(B) Pain
(C) Pelvic
(D) Ectopic pregnancy
A: Bleeding

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Duffy blood group antigen negativity confers protection against infection by: September 2012
(A) Plasmodium falciparum
(B) Plasmodium vivax
(C) Plasmodium ovate
(D) Plasmodium malariae
B: Plasmodium vivax

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Each milliliter of red cells contains ....... mg of elemental iron:
(A) 1 mg
(B) 2 mg
(C) 3 mg
(D) 4 mg
A: 1 mg

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Bence jones proteins are -
(A) Heavy chain IgG
(B) Present in bone marrow
(C) Seen in lymphoma
(D) Light chain IgG
D: Light chain IgG

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Mutation in which of the following chromosome is associated with meningioma ?
(A) Chromosome 1
(B) Chromosome 19
(C) Chromosome 22
(D) Chromosome 9q
C: Chromosome 22

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Hyoglossus muscle inserts into
(A) Tip of the tongue
(B) Base of the tongue
(C) Lateral part of the tongue
(D) Hyoid bone
C: Lateral part of the tongue

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

1st evidence of calcification of Primary mandibular central incisor is seen in
(A) 14 wk IU
(B) 16 wk IU
(C) 17 wk IU
(D) 15 wk IU
A: 14 wk IU

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

If C5 ,C6 nerve root is injured, sensations of which pa of upper limb will be lost ?
(A) Thumb and index finger
(B) Little finger
(C) Upper medial pa of arm
(D) Upper medial side of forearm
A: Thumb and index finger

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All are steroids except ?
(A) Testosterone
(B) Vitamin D
(C) Cholesterol
(D) Thyroxine
D: Thyroxine

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All of the following drugs are metabolised by acetylation except
(A) INH
(B) Sulfonamides
(C) Ketoconazole
(D) Hydralazine
C: Ketoconazole

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Nail's involvement is NOT a feature of: March 2003
(A) Psoriasis
(B) DLE
(C) Dermatomyositis
(D) Lichen planus
B: DLE

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Iopanoic acid is used in -
(A) Myelography
(B) Bronchography
(C) Hysterosalpingography
(D) Cholecystography
D: Cholecystography

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Primary atypical pneumonia is caused by
(A) Legionella
(B) Streptococcus
(C) Mycoplasma
(D) Listeria
C: Mycoplasma

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A 32-year-old woman has had a chronic cough with fever for the past month. On physical examination, her temperature is 37.5deg C. A chest radiograph shows many small, ill-defined nodular opacities in all lung fields. A transbronchial biopsy specimen shows interstitial infiltrates with lymphocytes, plasma cells, and epithelioid macrophages. Which of the following infectious agents is the most likely cause of this appearance?
(A) Candida albicans
(B) Cytomegalovirus
(C) Enterobacter aerogenes
(D) Mycobacterium tuberculosis
D: Mycobacterium tuberculosis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

20-year-old female present with features of anemia. Blood tests: Hb-5g/dL, MCV - 52 fL, MCH-22 pg, PCV - 15%. Diagnosis?
(A) Phenytoin toxicity
(B) Fish tapeworm infection
(C) Hookworm infection
(D) Blind loop syndrome
C: Hookworm infection

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Ruffled membrane is seen in -
(A) White fibrous cailage
(B) Osteoblast
(C) Osteoclast
(D) Elastic cailage
C: Osteoclast

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which color plastic bag is used for noninfectious waste?
(A) White
(B) Yellow
(C) Red
(D) Black
D: Black

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A 55-year-old man with recent onset of atrial fibrillation presents with a cold, pulseless left lower extremity. He complains of left leg paresthesia and is unable to dorsiflex his toes. Following a successful popliteal embolectomy, with restoration of palpable pedal pulses, the patient is still unable to dorsi- flex his toes. The next step in management should be
(A) Electromyography (EMG)
(B) Measurement of anterior compartment pressure
(C) Elevation of the left leg
(D) Immediate fasciotomy
D: Immediate fasciotomy

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All of these are derivative of neural crest except
(A) Melanocyte
(B) Adrenal medulla
(C) Sympathetic ganglia
(D) Cauda equina
D: Cauda equina

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Substrate level phosphorylation is catalysed by :
(A) Succinate dehydrogenase
(B) Thiokinase
(C) Malate dehydrogenase
(D) Hexokinase
B: Thiokinase

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

mood stabilizer used in the tretament of rapid cycling
(A) vaproate
(B) lithium
(C) carbamezepine
(D) lamotrigine
A: vaproate

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

LCAT is induced by ?
(A) Apo A-I
(B) Apo B-48
(C) Apo B-100
(D) Apo C-II
A: Apo A-I

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Musculocutaneous nerve is injured at the lateral cord of brachial plexus, positive clinical findings would be:
(A) Loss of flexion at shoulder
(B) Sensory loss on the radial side of the forearm
(C) Loss of extension of forearm
(D) Loss of extension of the wrist
B: Sensory loss on the radial side of the forearm

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Conversion of Pyruvate to Acetyl CoA does not require
(A) Thiamine
(B) TPP
(C) Pyridoxine
(D) FAD
C: Pyridoxine

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Roseola infantum is caused by (most commonly)-
(A) HHV - 2
(B) HHV - 6
(C) HHV - 7
(D) HHV - 8
B: HHV - 6

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A patient presents with symptoms of Hypoglycemia. Investigations reveal decreased blood glucose and increased Insulin levels. C-peptide assay is done which shows normal levels of C-peptide. The most likely diagnosis is:
(A) Insulinoma
(B) Accidental sulfonylurea ingestion
(C) Accidental exogenous Insulin administration
(D) Accidental Metformin ingestion
C: Accidental exogenous Insulin administration

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Most accurate test among the following:
(A) Heat
(B) Cold
(C) EPT.
(D) All of the above.
B: Cold

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Recommended daily dose of Iodine in a child is:-
(A) 120-200 ug
(B) 90-120 ug
(C) 30-60 ug
(D) 500 ug

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which index is used to score the outcome of treatment in patients with cleft lip and palate?
(A) Index of Orthodontic Treatment Complexity (IOTC)
(B) Summer’s Index
(C) Index of Complexity Outcome and Need (ICON)
(D) Goslon Yardstick
D: Goslon Yardstick

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All are true for transplanted kidney except -
(A) Humoral antibody responsible for rejection
(B) CMI is responsible for rejection
(C) Previous blood transfusion
(D) HLA identity similarity seen in 1:100 people
D: HLA identity similarity seen in 1:100 people

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Drug of choice for the treatment of negative symptoms of schizophrenia is -
(A) Chlorpromazine
(B) Haloperidol
(C) Clozapine
(D) Doxepin
C: Clozapine

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Isolated hyperbilirubinemia is classically seen as an adverse effect of:
(A) INH
(B) Rifampin
(C) Pyrazinamide
(D) Capreomycin
B: Rifampin

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which of the following is a prokinetic drug?
(A) Domperidone
(B) Cimetidine
(C) Ondansetron
(D) Hyoscine
A: Domperidone

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All are seen in VKH syndrome except -
(A) Vitiligo
(B) Veigo
(C) Granulomatous uveitis
(D) Iris heterochromia
D: Iris heterochromia

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Plasma half life of carbimazole -
(A) 4 hours
(B) 8 hours
(C) 16 hours
(D) 24 hours
B: 8 hours

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Testamentary capacity refers to
(A) Ability to make a valid will
(B) Criminal liability
(C) Right to vote
(D) Ability to give evidence
A: Ability to make a valid will

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Sjogren syndrome is characterized by all except
(A) Keratoconjunctivitis sicca
(B) Excessive salivation
(C) Splenomegaly
(D) Lymphoma
B: Excessive salivation

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Neurovascular plane in the anterior abdominal wall is between
(A) External and internal oblique
(B) Internal oblique and transversus abdominis
(C) Peritoneum and transversalis fascia
(D) Skin and external oblique aponeurosis
B: Internal oblique and transversus abdominis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Most common cause of Mediastinitis is:
(A) Tracheal rupture
(B) Esophageal rupture
(C) Drugs
(D) Idiopathic
B: Esophageal rupture

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Tension band wiring is done in following except?
(A) Fracture patella
(B) Fracture olecranon
(C) Colie's Fracture
(D) Fracture medial malleolus
C: Colie's Fracture

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

% of O2 inhaled-
(A) 5%
(B) 20%
(C) 50%
(D) 78%

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

True about inspiration; the intrapleural pressure becomes:
(A) More - ye
(B) More +ve
(C) Same
(D) Initially positive, then negative
A: More - ye

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All the following are true of de Quervain's Thyroiditis except -
(A) Pain
(B) Increased ESR
(C) Increased radioactive iodine uptake
(D) Fever
C: Increased radioactive iodine uptake

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

In which condition intensity of systolic murmur is incre ased-
(A) Hyperophic cardiomathy
(B) Severe AS
(C) MS
(D) AR
B: Severe AS

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

At 30 days of intrauterine life
(A) Hea stas beating
(B) Cerebellum develops
(C) Optical vesicle appears
(D) Pinna appears
C: Optical vesicle appears

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Vogt-Koyanagi-Harada (VKH) syndrome is:
(A) Chronic granulomatous uveitis
(B) Chronic non-granulomatous uveitis
(C) Acute purulent uveitis
(D) None
A: Chronic granulomatous uveitis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A 36-year-old diabetic woman develops metabolic changes following salpingo-oophorectomy. Serum osmolality of the blood can be calculated from serum values of which of the following?
(A) Sodium, potassium, chloride, and bicarbonate
(B) Sodium, potassium, urea, and hemoglobin
(C) Sodium, potassium, glucose, and urea
(D) Sodium, albumin, urea, and glucose
C: Sodium, potassium, glucose, and urea

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

The cause of oedema in Nephritic syndrome is
(A) Decreased in plasma protein concentration
(B) Increased in plasma protein concentration
(C) Reduced plasma osmotic pressure
(D) Sodium and water retension
D: Sodium and water retension

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

An infant present with bilateral white pupillary reflex. On slit lamp examination a zone of opacity is observed arround the fetal nucleus with spoke like radial opacities. The most likely diagnosis is-
(A) Cataracta Centralis Pulverulenta
(B) Lamellar cataract
(C) Coronary cataract
(D) Posterior polar cataract
B: Lamellar cataract

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

What type of hypersensitivity reaction is seen in myasthenia gravis?
(A) Type 1 hypersensitivty reaction
(B) Type 2 hypersensitivty reaction
(C) Type 3 hypersensitivty reaction
(D) Type 4 hypersensitivty reaction
B: Type 2 hypersensitivty reaction

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Aery to bleed in duodenal ulcer haemorrhage -
(A) Splenic aery
(B) Gastroduodenal aery
(C) Left gastric aery
(D) Sup. mesenteric aery
B: Gastroduodenal aery

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Gustilo Anderson classification is used for ?
(A) Compound fractures
(B) Closed fractures
(C) Distal end radius fractures
(D) Femur head fractures
A: Compound fractures

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Day 3 serum Estrogen levels to diagnose premature ovarian failure:
(A) <10-20 pg/ ml
(B) <20-40 pg/ ml
(C) <60-80 pg /ml
(D) <40-60 pg /ml

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which antineoplastic agent is an antifolate drug ?
(A) Methotrexate
(B) Adriamycin
(C) Vincristine
(D) Cyclophosphamide
A: Methotrexate

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

An early systolic murmur may be caused by all of the following, EXCEPT:
(A) Small ventricular septal defect
(B) Papillary muscle dysfunction
(C) Tricuspid regurgitation
(D) Aoic stenosis
D: Aoic stenosis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Gold standard diagnostic test for babeiosis is:
(A) Peripheral blood smear examination
(B) Blood Culture
(C) PCR
(D) ELISA
A: Peripheral blood smear examination

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Three years back a woman had exposure to an eahquake and she was successfully saved. After recovery she has nightmares about the episode and she also gets up in the night and feels terrified. The MOST probable diagnosis is :
(A) Major depression
(B) Post-traumatic stress disorder
(C) Mania
(D) Schizophrenia
B: Post-traumatic stress disorder

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Single lesion leprosy treatment
(A) Dapsone and rifampicin
(B) Dapsone and clofazamine and ofloxacin
(C) Rifampicin ,ofloxacin and dapsone
(D) Rifampicin , olfoxacin and minocycline
D: Rifampicin , olfoxacin and minocycline

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which polyp has maximum malignant potential ?
(A) Sessile
(B) Pedunculated
(C) Superficial spreading
(D) Any of the above
A: Sessile

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All of the following are contraindications to use diaphragm except :
(A) Multiple sex paners
(B) Recurrent infections
(C) Uterine prolapse
(D) Herpes vaginitis
A: Multiple sex paners

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Cholinergic drugs are used in all the following conditions except
(A) Cobra Bite
(B) Post operative paralytic ileus
(C) Glaucoma
(D) Bradycardia
D: Bradycardia

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Undulant fever is caused by
(A) Salmonella typhi
(B) Staphylococcus aureus
(C) Brucella melitensis
(D) Coxiella burnetti
C: Brucella melitensis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

All are true about hyperophic cardiomyopathy, except?
(A) Systolic dysfunction
(B) Dynamic left ventricular outflow tract obstruction
(C) Diastolic dysfunction
(D) Double apical impulse
A: Systolic dysfunction

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

The following regarding colostomy are true except?
(A) A colostomy is an aificial opening made in large Bowel to dive the faeces to the exterior
(B) Temporary colostomy is established to defunction an anastomosi
(C) Permanent colostomy is formed after the resection of Rectum by the abdominoperinea technique
(D) Double barreled colostomy is commonly done nowadays
D: Double barreled colostomy is commonly done nowadays

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Dysthyroid ophthalmopathy all are true except-
(A) Proptosis
(B) Myopathy
(C) Exophthalmos
(D) Optic neuritis
D: Optic neuritis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

In which of the following conditions fluid levels are NOT seen?
(A) Meconium ileus
(B) Intussusception
(C) Colon pouch
(D) Duodenal obstruction
A: Meconium ileus

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Among the given options ; iodinated compound present in maximum concentration in thyroid is
(A) Monoiodotyrosine (MIT).
(B) Diiodotyrosine (DIT).
(C) T3.
(D) Reverse T3.
B: Diiodotyrosine (DIT).

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Calcific hepatic metastasis are seen in
(A) Adenocarcinoma of the colon
(B) Carcinoid tumors
(C) Renal cell carcinoma
(D) Lymphoma
A: Adenocarcinoma of the colon

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which waves is predominantly seen in EEG with eyes closed in normal alert adult?
(A) Alpha waves
(B) Beta waves
(C) Theta waves
(D) Delta waves
A: Alpha waves

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A 63-year-old woman develops skin lesions, and difficulty getting out of a chair. A clinical diagnosis of dermatomyositis is made. What might the findings include?For the above patient, select the associated skin and clinical findings.
(A) rarely involves border of scalp
(B) discoloration of upper eyelids
(C) never a permanent effect on skin
(D) potentially aggravated by contact with the skin
B: discoloration of upper eyelids

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which among the following is the MOST common site of salivary gland calculi formation?
(A) Parotid
(B) Submandibular
(C) Sublingual
(D) Minor salivary glands
B: Submandibular

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A 5-month old infant is brought in for routine visit. What is the probable cause for the following findings?
(A) Benign skin lesion
(B) Sepsis
(C) Hemophilia
(D) Child abuse
A: Benign skin lesion

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Tumor appears at the earliest after birth.
(A) Cystic hygroma
(B) Branchial cyst
(C) Lymphoma
(D) Sternomastoid tumor
A: Cystic hygroma

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Sensory supply of middle ear is provided by
(A) Facial
(B) Glossopharyngeal
(C) Vagus
(D) Trigeminal
B: Glossopharyngeal

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

As per 'Rule of Nine', each lower limb accounts for how much % of total body surface area:
(A) 1%
(B) 9%
(C) 18%
(D) 27%

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Patient with massive hemorrhage presents to the ER after A. What's not to be done?
(A) Massive transfusion of fluid challenge
(B) IV crystalloid 2L, within few minutes
(C) Early tranexamic acid recommended
(D) Check coagulation with thromboelastography, if available
B: IV crystalloid 2L, within few minutes

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

A client is undergoing peritoneal dialysis. The dialysate dwell time is completed, and the dwell clamp is opened to allow the dialysate to drain. The nurse notes that the drainage has stopped and only 500 ml has drained; the amount the dialysate instilled was 1,500 ml. Which of the following interventions would be done first?
(A) Change the client’s position.
(B) Call the physician
(C) Check the catheter for kinks or obstruction.
(D) Clamp the catheter and instill more dialysate at the next exchange time
C: Check the catheter for kinks or obstruction.

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Hirustism may be found in any of these disorders, except-
(A) Cushing's syndrome
(B) Hypothyroidism
(C) Congenital adrenal hyperplasia
(D) Polycystic ovarian syndrome
B: Hypothyroidism

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Trachoma is caused by which serotype of chlamydia trachomatis?
(A) D to K
(B) A, B, C
(C) L1 L2 L3
(D) All of the above
B: A, B, C

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Which hormone inhibits FSH secretion in feedback-
(A) Testosterone
(B) Progesterone
(C) Inhibin
(D) None
C: Inhibin

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Metrifonate is effective against -
(A) Amoebiasis
(B) Leishmaniosis
(C) Schistosomiasis
(D) Giardiasis
C: Schistosomiasis

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

Failure of rupture of bucconasal membrane leads to?
(A) Choanal atresia
(B) Rhinophyma
(C) Crooked nose
(D) Epistaxis
A: Choanal atresia

Answer this medical multiple choice question. Respond with only the letter (A/B/C/D).

AML is characterized by -
(A) Philadelphia chromosome
(B) Auer rods
